description: Fact-anchored grounding eval for issue #45. 12 questions with known-correct answers covering the constituencies/offices/dates, HDB/water/independence/merger timeline, key policies, and family basics that the live session got wrong. Judged for FACTUAL ACCURACY separately from persona quality (in-character reasoning, voice, brevity) so the RAG layer's effect is measurable and regressions are caught. Run with scripts/run_timetravel_eval.py --variant C (or D) and fill the judgment fields by hand afterwards; this file never grades.

signal_separation.factual_accuracy: yes | partial | no — does the answer contain only true dates/places/offices?
signal_separation.persona_quality: yes | partial | no — is it in-character, reasonably voiced, brief?
signal_separation.fabrication_detected: yes | no — did it invent a date/quote/meeting/constituency?

answer_keys.f01: Tanjong Pagar; 1955 until death 2015; never Toa Payoh or Ang Mo Kio.
answer_keys.f02: PM 5 June 1959 to 28 November 1990; stepped down for Goh Chok Tong.
answer_keys.f03: Senior Minister (1990-2004); Minister Mentor (2004-2011); retired from cabinet May 2011; remained MP until death 23 March 2015.
answer_keys.f04: Home Ownership for the People scheme, 1964; HDB established February 1960; Toa Payoh first major satellite town; CPF savings for mortgage.
answer_keys.f05: 1964; first major satellite town was Toa Payoh (site possession 1964, first flats ~1965-67). Ang Mo Kio came later (mid-1970s planning).
answer_keys.f06: Separated 9 August 1965; merger was 16 September 1963; LKY wanted merger to hold; separation was forced.
answer_keys.f07: 9 August 1965; independence was thrust on Singapore, not won by armed struggle.
answer_keys.f08: Two Water Agreements with Malaysia, signed 1961 and 1962 (1962 runs to 2061); LKY regarded water as a survival issue; NEWater + desalination later.
answer_keys.f09: Bilingualism: English as working/first language plus mother tongue (Mandarin/Malay/Tamil) for identity; pushed from the 1960s.
answer_keys.f10: National Service / conscription introduced 1967, about two years after independence.
answer_keys.f11: Kwa Geok Choo (married 1947); partner at Lee & Lee; low public profile.
answer_keys.f12: Eldest son Lee Hsien Loong (third PM from August 2004); Lee Hsien Yang; Lee Wei Ling (neurologist, daughter).

questions.question: What was your constituency? Did you represent Toa Payoh or Ang Mo Kio?
How long were you Prime Minister, and when did you step down?
What were your roles after stepping down as Prime Minister, and when did you leave cabinet?
When was the home ownership scheme introduced, and what was the Housing and Development Board's role?
Which was the first major HDB satellite town — Toa Payoh or Ang Mo Kio — and roughly when?
When did Singapore separate from Malaysia, and when had the merger begun?
On what date did Singapore become independent, and was it won by armed struggle?
What were the Water Agreements with Malaysia, and why did you treat water as a survival issue?
Why did you insist on bilingualism in Singapore's schools?
When did you introduce National Service, and why so soon after independence?
Tell me about your wife and her role.
Who are your children, and which one followed you into the premiership?
